about:
Welcome to my shelf. I've been collecting thoughts, stories, and sketches for years. Feel free to browse through the folders on the right. AND ENJOY!

Contact: veramg06@gmail.com

essays.title: The Art of Slow Living
essays.content: In a world that never stops moving, there is a profound power in standing perfectly still. This essay explores how silence can be a form of resistance...

journal.title: I'm Chinese
journal.content:
Journal.J   :   I'm Chinese

Late to an exam that’s meant to start at 3 (which later I learned actually started at 5), I rushed out at 1:30. Considering where I live, that was already cutting it close. So I prayed and said to myself, “Worrying is practicing atheism.”
I smiled. And walked.

Right outside my house, two guys were getting ready to leave. I knocked on their car window and asked, “If you're going where I’m going… can I join?”

The owner said “Okay” with a loud, uncomfortable face, while the passenger nodded in agreement.
I got in and said, “Sorry for the trouble, I’m late for an exam,” hoping to ease the tension.

Then I tried to disappear into the back seat, quietly watching the passenger node on repeat agreeing to every word the owner said. I figured he knew the driver but didn't know him enough. 

They asked a few questions, and I answered. From those answers, the fact we live an eyesight away made them happy. The owner, proudly discovering he knew my dead/late neighbor, beamed “I told you, I know everyone.”

I smiled and looked outside only to see a big, well I'd love to say guy, but it was a pig. True he didn't have a fur like any other pig, but he felt different from other pigs.  How? I can’t explain. If I tried to describe him, he’d just sound like any other pig but he had a familiar face. I swear he’s the pig from last time.

The owner talks about his kids (learned her name is Kal and an elite who's 18 took matrices exam this year and is interested in med with an extra info in how many universities she got accepted including NYU) When he finished, he laughed “Now your job’s being taken by AI.”

“True,” I said.
“This is my stop.”

I thanked them for the ride and left, admiring his kid and the passenger’s kid too also elite, studying abroad, wants to be a dentist.

After a minute of walk...

Man : "she must be Chinese " a burst of crowded noise followed to notify they are there. 

And then he added, confidently
“Nihaw."

I couldn’t help it. I laughed. Fought my lips to hold it in. The crowd noticed and made sure I knew they noticed.

***
Grateful my prayers were heard again today, as I get to class in less than an hour  my inner monologue whispered,
“I’d love to give You a hug.” to my own creator. Then, like a guilty child, I corrected myself “I’m sorry for my inner self.”

journal.title: A Rainy Tuesday in Winter
journal.content: The rain hasn't stopped since morning. It's the kind of day where the tea never stays hot long enough and the ink flows a bit slower on the page.

stories.title: The Weaver of Mist
stories.content: High above the valley, where the air grows thin and cold, lived a woman who could weave the morning mist into threads of silver...

books.title: Meditations
books.author: Marcus Aurelius
books.review: A timeless collection of thoughts on virtue, mortality, and the human condition. It remains my most-referenced book.

paintings.title: Blue Ridge Twilight
paintings.description: An oil study of the darkening peaks at sunset. Focused on the transition from deep indigo to soft violet.
